title: Japan advances to World Cup round of 16 after draw with Sweden and prepares to face Brazil
summary: The Japanese team secures its place in the knockout stage and now prepares for the match against the five-time world champions.

title: Typhoon No. 8 and heavy rains cause flooding and paralyze transportation in Japan
summary: Authorities warn of the risk of landslides and urge caution as the storm advances.

title: Japan plans to strengthen age verification on social media to protect minors
summary: A group of experts proposes sanctions against companies that fail to meet new user identification requirements.

title: Taxis using light vehicles begin operating in Fukuoka, a first in Japan
summary: The service, with capacity for three passengers, aims to attract more drivers, especially women and young people.

title: Emperors of Japan return from official visit to the Netherlands and Belgium
summary: The imperial couple highlighted the strengthening of friendship and cooperation between the nations during their European tour.

title: Bill against destruction of national flag approved in parliamentary committee
summary: The initiative, criticized by the opposition and the cultural community, moves to the plenary of the Upper House with a possibility of being enacted.

title: Nissan recalls more than 600,000 vehicles due to defects in the Note and other models
summary: The Japanese company issues a massive recall affecting three models, without specifying the specific problems.

title: Nikkei index suffers its biggest drop of the year, losing more than 3,000 points in one day
summary: The stock market slump, attributed to profit-taking and global uncertainty, puts the index at 69,000 yen.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 230 dead and 4,300 injured
summary: The quake, which caused the collapse of numerous buildings, also triggered 40,000 reports of missing persons on the search platform.

title: Justice minister apologizes for using mobile phone during Parliament session
summary: Hiraguchi admits to having 'forgotten to silence the phone' while answering questions about the process review law.

title: Prime Minister Takai meets with IAEA chief to discuss Iranian nuclear crisis
summary: Both leaders reaffirm the need for on-site inspections to verify Iran's nuclear program.

title: Honda faces criticism at shareholders' meeting and announces imminent deal with Nissan
summary: Honda's president reveals that collaboration with Nissan is about to be formalized, as investors push for management changes.

title: Apple and Calbee raise prices in Japan due to rising costs
summary: The iPad price increase reaches 25%, while Calbee snacks go up between 3% and 15% due to inflation.

title: Matchups set for high school baseball prefectural tournaments in five regions
summary: The federations of Fukui, Yamagata, Akita, Iwate, and Fukushima draw the first-round matches of the summer competition.

title: China accuses Japan of 'harassment' during Chinese aircraft carrier drills in the Pacific
summary: The Chinese government protests the presence of Japanese ships and aircraft near its battle group on the high seas.

title: Secretary to Chief Cabinet Secretary Mogi dismissed over alleged misuse of public funds
summary: The official's departure follows revelations that he allegedly used official money for a personal trip with a lover.

title: Centrist, Constitutional Democratic, and Komeito parties begin merger negotiations
summary: Talks begin five months after the creation of the centrist party, in an attempt to unify the opposition.

title: Chinese residents in Japan fear stricter visa rules may force many to leave the country
summary: New rules for renewing residency permits could force the departure of Chinese citizens who meet current requirements.

title: Journalist from independent media remains in Hong Kong to report despite restrictions
summary: The reporter continues his work in the semi-autonomous city, resisting pressure from the Chinese government to avoid covering sensitive topics.

title: Japan's National Film Archive turns to crowdfunding after drastic budget cut
summary: The institution aims to raise 100 million yen to maintain its operations amid cuts in public funding.
